data.title: Lufthansa Stock Soars on Asia Route Expansion as Middle East Conflict Shifts Demand
data.description:
Deutsche Lufthansa (DLAKY) (DE:LHA) shares closed 5.8% higher yesterday as the airline successfully captures a massive influx of new passengers. With conflict in the Middle East disrupting many major Gulf-based carriers, Lufthansa is moving its resources to fill the gap. The company's Chief Financial Officer, Till Streichert, confirmed that the airline is seeing a "significant increase in bookings" for March departures.

data.title: New York Times Is Still a "Battleground Stock," But This Top Analyst Is All In
data.description:
New York Times' (NYT) shares "remain a battleground stock" as analysts are split on the standing of the newspaper giant, according to Citi (C). However, the investment bank's five-star analyst Jason Bazinet remains bullish on the New York Times, citing the media heavyweight's robust digital pivot.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Citi Brushes Off the Bearish Noise

Following his latest assessment, Bazinet maintained his Buy rating on NYT stock and lifted his price target by 22%, from $77 to $94, implying about 14% upside. The analyst gave the bullish rating despite pointing to critics' arguments about missed key performance indicators and elevated multiples — that is, investors paying more per unit for fundamentals such as earnings and sales.

Apart from being a five-star-rated analyst, Bazinet ranks 371 out of the more than 12,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He boasts a 58% success rate and an average return of 14.70%.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SoFi Stock Slides 35% This Year — Is It Time to Buy the Dip?
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock remains under pressure, trading lower in premarket on Tuesday and down about 35% year-to-date. The decline follows a short-seller report from Muddy Waters that raised concerns about the company's business and accounting.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, CEO Anthony Noto bought nearly $500,000 worth of shares after the drop, signaling confidence. However, Wall Street remains cautious. SOFI stock carries a Hold consensus rating, even though the average price target of about $25 implies roughly 50% upside from current levels. This suggests that while the upside looks attractive, risks around valuation and recent concerns are keeping analysts on the sidelines.

Bull Case: Insider Confidence and Investor Support

SoFi's management has shown confidence at current levels, with CEO Anthony Noto stepping in and buying shares after the recent selloff.

Beyond that, broader insider activity has remained positive. Over the past three months, insiders have bought shares worth about $1.2 million, according to TipRanks' Insider Trading Activity Tool. This steady buying suggests that those closest to the company remain confident in its long-term outlook.

More importantly, retail investor sentiment also remains supportive. TipRanks data shows that about 2.9% of portfolios hold SOFI, with an average allocation of 5.59%. Holdings have increased by 0.2% over the past seven days and 3.1% over the last 30 days. Overall, investor sentiment is marked as "Positive," indicating continued support despite recent volatility.

data.title: Altman Drops Helion Board Seat to Clear Path for OpenAI's Biggest Power Deal Yet
data.description:
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has stepped down from the board of directors at Helion, the nuclear fusion startup he has backed since 2015. His resignation comes as OpenAI moves toward a potential large-scale partnership with the company, raising the stakes on its energy ambitions. 

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Helion Could Be OpenAI's Biggest Energy Bet Yet

Helion CEO David Kirtley confirmed Altman's departure, saying the move "enables Helion and OpenAI to partner on future opportunities to bring zero-carbon, safe electricity to the world." The reported deal could see OpenAI secure 12.5% of Helion's total electricity output, amounting to 5 gigawatts by 2030 and 50 gigawatts by 2035. 

Helion has not confirmed any new customer agreements beyond existing deals with Microsoft (MSFT) and Nucor (NUE). This move mirrors Altman's April 2025 resignation from the board of nuclear fission startup Oklo (OKLO), which was also done to clear the path for potential OpenAI energy contracts.

Additionally, OpenAI's latest energy deal plan follows a trend among big tech companies like Microsoft, Alphabet (GOOGL), and Amazon (AMZN), striking long-term deals with nuclear power operators and developers. Altman expressed continued enthusiasm for Helion, noting he remains "extremely excited about a future with abundant energy".

Altman Exit Reinforces OpenAI's Energy Ambition

Altman's board exit is directly tied to OpenAI's growing energy ambitions. The company's 2025 Stargate initiative, a joint venture with SoftBank (SFTBY) and Oracle (ORCL) targeting 10 gigawatts of artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure capacity, represents over $400 billion in planned investment.   

As of early 2026, OpenAI says it is already "well beyond halfway" to that goal in planned capacity. Powering that scale of infrastructure reliably and cleanly is an unsolved problem, and a Helion partnership could be Altman's proposed answer. 

Altman, who also sits on the OpenAI board, said the dual roles had become a conflict of interest as the two companies inch closer to a potential energy supply agreement. In his X post, he confirmed he will retain a financial stake in Helion but recuse himself from any deal negotiations. 

Does OpenAI Have Any Stock?

OpenAI does not have a stock, as it remains a private company until its anticipated initial public offering (IPO) in 2026. For investors seeking exposure to AI companies, TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center highlights top-rated names with "Strong Buy" ratings, including Nvidia (NVDA), Micron (MU), GOOGL, Meta (META), and MSFT.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Why Is Paranovus Entertainment Technology Stock (PAVS) Up Today?
data.description:
Paranovus Entertainment Technology (PAVS) stock rocketed higher on Tuesday after the AI entertainment company revealed a stock sale update in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The company said that it ended a stock sale agreement with A.G.P/Alliance Global Partners that went into effect on October 28, 2025.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paranovus Entertainment Technology noted that it sent a termination notice to A.G.P/Alliance Global Partners on March 18 to end this sale agreement. This termination went into effect on Sunday. The company sold 5,880,052 shares of PAVS stock via this sales agreement, which was adjusted for its one-for-100 reverse stock split that went into effect on December 18, 2025.

The end of the stock sale acted as a catalyst to boost investor morale in Paranovus Entertainment Technology. This makes sense as further share sales would dilute the stakes of investors in the company. With the sale over, shareholders can remain confident that their investment in PAVS won't be diluted by additional stock sales from the sales agreement.

Paranovus Entertainment Technology Stock Surges Today

Paranovus Entertainment Technology stock was up 109.89% in premarket trading on Tuesday, following a 3.55% fall yesterday. The shares have decreased 82.87% year-to-date and 99.78% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of PAVS stock, as more than 99 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 440,000 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Shareholders Sue Over BlackRock TCP's NAV Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against BlackRock TCP Capital Corp (TCPC) on February 3, 2026.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Plaintiffs in the federal securities class action allege that they acquired BlackRock TCP stock at artificially inflated prices between November 6, 2024, and January 23, 2026, known as the "Class Period." They are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged misconduct during that time. To learn whether you may be eligible for a recovery under this securities lawsuit, click here.

What Does BlackRock TCP Do?

  • It is a self-described externally-managed specialty finance company.
  • As such, BlackRock TCP concentrates its efforts on middle-market lending. Specifically, its goal is to secure "high total returns through current income and capital appreciation, with an emphasis on principal protection." To that end, the company explains, the bulk of its investments are in debt of private, middle-market U.S. companies. In most cases, these companies have enterprise values ranging from typically $100 million to $1.5 billion. Some of these investments involve businesses with "complex situations requiring specialized industry knowledge."
  • According to the complaint, one of the company's most important financial metrics for investors is net asset value or "NAV." This is defined in the complaint  as the value of the company's underlying assets, minus their total liabilities. The company's claims about its NAV are at the crux of the current lawsuit.

Why are TCPC Shareholders Suing the Company?

The company and three of its current and/or former senior officers (the "Individual Defendants") are now accused of deceiving investors by lying and withholding important information about BlackRock TCP's business practices and prospects during the Class Period.

Specifically, they are accused of omitting truthful information about the ways in which investments are valued, and effects on its NAV, from SEC filings and related material. By knowingly or recklessly doing so, they allegedly caused BlackRock TCP stock to trade at artificially inflated prices during the time in question. The company first disclosed a decrease in its NAV in a press release issued  before the market opened on February 27, 2025.

Less than a year later, after the market closed on January 23, 2026, the company issued another press release about certain fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results. In this context, BlackRock TCP disclosed that its NAV per share as of December 31, 2025 "was, in fact, in the range of $7.05 to $7.09, 19% less than reported the prior quarter and 23.4% less than reported the prior year."

Taking a Closer Look

As alleged, BlackRock TCP and/or the Individual Defendants repeatedly made false and misleading public statements throughout the Class Period.

In a press release issued at the beginning of the Class Period, for instance, the company stated in pertinent part: "Net asset value per share was $10.11 as of September 30, 2024 compared to $10.20 as of June 30, 2024."

In the same press release, the company stated: "At quarter end, our portfolio remained well diversified with 156 investments primarily in senior secured, first-lien loans. We have a strong capital and liquidity position to capitalize on a growing pipeline of attractive investment opportunities to deliver attractive risk-adjusted returns for our shareholders over the long term."

The company added: "Our portfolio showed signs of improvement since last quarter as nonaccrual investments declined; however, an additional non-accrual investment and certain markdowns resulted in a slight reduction in the NAV. We are working diligently with our borrowers, their lenders, and their sponsors to resolve credit issues with the goal of achieving positive outcomes for our shareholders."

Lastly, in a quarterly report filed with the SEC on November 6, 2024, BlackRock TCP stated in pertinent part: "All investments are valued at least quarterly based on quotations or other affirmative pricing from independent third-party sources, with the exception of investments priced directly by the Valuation Designee which in the aggregate comprise less than 5% of the assets of the Company."

Actions You May Take

If you have purchased the Company's stock during the Class Period, you may join the class action as a lead plaintiff, remain a passive class member, or opt out of this litigation and pursue individual claims that may not be available to the class as a whole. To learn more about your options, click here.

The deadline to file for lead plaintiff in this class action is April 6, 2026.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: 'Buy' Alibaba Stock, Reaffirms Morgan Stanley. Here's Why
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) remains a "Top Pick" at Morgan Stanley after the company unveiled its new XuanTie C950 AI chip, which is more than three times faster than its earlier version. The firm's analyst, Gary Yu, reiterated an Overweight rating and set a $180 price target, pointing to Alibaba's growing strength in artificial intelligence.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why the Analyst Is Bullish on Alibaba Stock

Yu said the new chip strengthens his positive view on Alibaba. He believes the company now has strong control over key parts of AI. The company now designs its own chips through its T-Head unit. This helps it rely less on outside suppliers and manage costs better. It also allows the company to scale faster when demand rises and reduces risks from U.S. export rules.

Morgan Stanley also pointed to Alibaba's full AI setup as a key strength. The company brings together chips, cloud, models, and applications. Alibaba Cloud provides the computing power, while its Qwen models and apps support wider use.

Yu added that tools like Wukong and the Alibaba Token Hub could help turn AI into revenue over time by expanding use across businesses and consumers.

The firm values the T-Head unit at $28 billion to $86 billion, or about $22 per share, as part of its broader $245 valuation.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now? 

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on eight Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $188.38, suggesting roughly 49.44% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Futures Trend Lower as Oil Prices Recover amid Continued Tensions in the Middle East
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning as oil prices moved higher, recovering from yesterday's decline following President Donald Trump's comments on "productive conversations" with Iran that eased geopolitical tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.26%, 0.26%, and 0.25%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EDT on March 24.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 2.98% to $102.92 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) moved higher by 4.11% to $91.75 per barrel.

During Monday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher amid renewed hopes for an imminent resolution to the U.S.-Iran standoff. The Dow and the Nasdaq each gained 1.38%, while the S&P 500 surged 1.15%.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of Netgear (NTGR), a maker of WiFi routers and network switches, jumped more than 11% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) banned all imports of consumer routers made abroad, citing national security risks.

Meanwhile, CoreWeave (CRWV) stock rose about 2% after Bank of America reinstated coverage of the AI cloud company and upgraded its rating to Buy from Hold, as it believes that the company is well-positioned to capture a share of the $79 billion AI infrastructure as a service (IaaS) market.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: VCX Fund Surges 317% in Debut – Access to Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX
data.description:
The Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) jumped 63% yesterday and has risen over 590% in its first three trading days since listing on the New York Stock Exchange on March 19, 2026. VCX gives retail investors direct access to private tech and AI companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, Databricks, SpaceX, Anduril, and Ramp. Unlike a traditional IPO, this structure prioritizes retail investors over institutions.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Managed by Fundrise, an SEC-registered non-diversified investment company since 2021, VCX had over 100,000 pre-listing investors and initial assets under management (AUM) exceeding $650 million. Its net asset value (NAV) entering trading was $18.97 per share.

VCX Democratizes Access to Private Tech

VCX opens ownership of top private companies to all retail traders. It targets high-growth private technology firms across stages, from early to late, and holds positions post-IPO when applicable. Key sectors include AI/machine learning, data infrastructure, fintech, aerospace, gaming, and related growth areas.

VCX's top five holdings include:

  • Anthropic (20.7%)
  • Databricks (17.7%)
  • OpenAI (9.9%)
  • Anduril (6.9%)
  • Ramp (5.1%)

If core unrestricted shareholders hold firm, scarcity could push prices above NAV; selling pressure might pull it below, akin to Robinhood Markets' (HOOD) Robinhood Ventures Fund I (RVI) debut.

Is VCX a Buy?

The Fundrise Innovation Fund has posted strong returns since launch. This track record comes from early bets on top private tech names like OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX.

We used TipRanks' Technical Analysis Tool to assess the outlook for the Fundrise Innovation Fund. The tool indicates a "Buy" consensus, with nine indicators showing Bullish signals and seven Bearish indicators.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
